# Imagined Inventory: vector-search-pipeline

**Project:** tradeguru-swift
**Created:** 2026-03-14
**Generated by:** `/imagine-inventory`
**Vision:** Vector store search + synthesis pipeline — query 127-page electrical knowledge base before generating chat responses, with real-time status indicators in the UI.

---

## Instructions

This is a **forward-looking TODO inventory** — items represent everything that needs to be built to deliver vector store search + synthesis for the TradeGuru v1 chat endpoint plus UI status indicators.

### Status definitions
| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `todo` | Not started — needs to be built |
| `in-progress` | Currently being worked on |
| `done` | Built and working — file path noted |
| `cut` | Descoped — decided not to build |
| `blocked` | Can't start until dependency is done |

## Build Order

### Phase 1 — Types + Config (foundation)
| # | Item | Type | Why first |
|---|------|------|-----------|
| 1 | VectorSearchTypes | backend-type | All backend services depend on these types |
| 2 | PipelineStatus type | backend-type | SSE status events need this shape defined |
| 3 | PipelineStage enum | swift-model | Swift UI depends on this |

### Phase 2 — Backend Services (vector search pipeline)
| # | Item | Type | Why now |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 1 | VectorStoreSearch | backend-service | Core search capability — must exist before chat route can use it |
| 2 | SearchResultFormatter | backend-service | Formats search results for injection into prompts |
| 3 | SynthesisStreamer (modify openai-responses.ts) | backend-modify | Accepts vector context in the streaming call |

### Phase 3 — Backend Integration (wire into chat route)
| # | Item | Type | Why now |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 1 | Chat route modify | backend-modify | Wire vector search → format → inject → stream pipeline. Add `event: status` SSE events. |
| 2 | Vision route modify | backend-modify | Same pattern as chat |

### Phase 4 — Swift UI (status indicators)
| # | Item | Type | Why now |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 1 | PipelineStatusDots | swift-view | Reusable animated dots component |
| 2 | PipelineStatusView | swift-view | Status bar using dots + stage text |
| 3 | ChatViewModel modify | swift-modify | Track pipeline stage, show/hide status view |

### Phase 5 — HTML Preview (must match Swift 1:1)
| # | Item | Type | Why now |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 1 | PipelineStatusBar + Dots | html-component | HTML replica of Swift PipelineStatusView |
| 2 | SSE parser — status events | html-modify | Parse `event: status` and drive the HTML status bar |

---

## Environment Requirements

| Variable | Required By | Where To Get It | Status |
|----------|------------|-----------------|--------|
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | VectorStoreSearch, openai-responses.ts | Already on Vercel | done |

**No new env vars needed** — the vector store search API uses the same OPENAI_API_KEY that chat completions uses. The vector store ID (`vs_69017d88924081919fc6599a18ae2231`) is hardcoded as a constant in the search service (same pattern as the existing `AssistantConfig.ts`).

---

## SSE Contract Addition

The pipeline adds one new SSE event type to the existing contract:

```
event: status
data: {"stage": "searching", "detail": "Querying knowledge base..."}

event: status
data: {"stage": "synthesizing", "detail": "Building response from 5 sources..."}

event: block
data: {"type": "warning", "content": "...", "severity": "high"}

event: block
data: {"type": "text", "content": "..."}

event: done
data: {"response_id": "resp_...", "usage": {...}, "cached": false}
```

Clients that don't understand `event: status` can safely ignore it (graceful degradation).

---

## Key Technical Decisions

1. **Raw fetch(), not OpenAI SDK** — Edge Runtime compatibility confirmed. SDK support is unverifiable per Codex factcheck.
2. **`developer` role for context injection** — OpenAI docs prefer `developer` over `system` for gpt-4o.
3. **Vector search only for fault_finder + learn modes** — Research mode uses web search (different knowledge source).
4. **Vector context as developer message** — Injected before system prompt so the model sees: developer (use these sources) → system (Trade Guru identity) → user messages.
5. **`max_num_results: 5`** — Balance between context quality and token cost. Configurable per mode.
6. **`rewrite_query: true`** — Let OpenAI optimize the search query for better retrieval.
7. **Status events are optional** — Old clients that don't parse `event: status` still work.

---
